zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Grey於1302年出生在英國德比夏爾可德諾時，她的父親Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray21歲，母親Joan Fitz Payne22歲。她於大約1318年與William de Harcourt結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1369年7月22日在英國斯塔福爾艾倫哈爾享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Grey于1302年出生在英国德比夏爾可德諾时，她的父亲Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray21岁，母亲Joan Fitz Payne22岁。她于大约1318年与William de Harcourt结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1369年7月22日在英国斯塔福爾艾倫哈爾享年67岁，埋葬在英国英格兰斯塔福爾。

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Grey was born in 1302, in Codnor, Derbyshire, England, her father, Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray, was 21 and her mother, Joan Fitz Payne, was 22. She married William de Harcourt about 1318. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 22 July 1369, in Ellenhall, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 67, and was buried in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Grey est née en 1302, à Codnor, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Joan Fitz Payne, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé William de Harcourt environ 1318. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 22 juillet 1369, à Ellenhall, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterrée à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat um 1318 William de Harcourt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 22. Juli 1369 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Ellenhall, Staffordshire, England und wurde in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Greyが1302年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアコドノーで生まれた時，父Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Grayは21歳，母Joan Fitz Payneは22歳であった。約1318年にWilliam de Harcourtと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1369年7月22日にイギリススタフォードシュアイアエレンハルで67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Grey(이)가 1302년 영국 더비실르 코드너에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Fitz Payne(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1318년 William de Harcourt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1369년 7월 22일 영국 스태퍼드실르 엘렌홀에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com William de Harcourt aproximadamente 1318. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Grey родилась в 1302 г. в местности коднор, дирбишир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard de Grey 2nd Baron de Gray было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Joan Fitz Payne, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Harcourt около 1318 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 22 июля 1369 г. в местности элленолл, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 67 лет и была похоронена в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
